content:
# Colossians Chapter 2 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/COL_002_page_001.jpg, COL_002_page_002.jpg

1. For I want you to know how great a struggle I have on your behalf and [on behalf of] those in Laodicea and as many as have not seen my face in [the] flesh,

2. that their hearts may be encouraged, having been knit together in love, and unto all the riches of the full assurance of the understanding, unto [the] knowledge of the mystery of God, [that is,] Christos,

3. in whom are all the treasures of the wisdom and of the knowledge hidden.

4. This I say so that no one may deceive you with persuasive speech.

5. For even if in the flesh I am absent, yet in the spirit I am with you, rejoicing and seeing your order and the firmness of your faith in Christos.

6. Therefore, as you received the Christos Yeshua the Lord, walk in him,

7. having been rooted and being built up in him and being established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in it with thanksgiving.

8. Watch out lest anyone takes you captive through the philosophy and empty deceit, according to the tradition of men, according to the elements of the world, and not according to Christos.

9. Because in him dwells all the fullness of the deity bodily,

10. and you are made full in him, who is the head of every ruler and authority.

11. In whom also you were circumcised with a circumcision not made by hands, in the putting off of the body of the flesh, in the circumcision of the Christos,

12. having been buried with him in the baptism, in which also you were raised with [him] through the faith of the working of God, the [one] having raised him from [the] dead.

13. And you, being dead in the trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, he made alive together with him, having forgiven us all the trespasses,

14. having wiped out the handwriting in the decrees against us, which was contrary to us, and he has taken it out of the midst, having nailed it to the cross.

15. Having stripped the rulers and the authorities, he made a public display [of them], triumphing over them in it.

16. Therefore let no one judge you in eating and in drinking, or in respect of a feast or a new moon or sabbaths,

17. which are a shadow of the things to come, but the body [is] of the Christos.

18. Let no one disqualify you, delighting in humility and worship of the angels, entering into things he has seen, puffed up without cause by the mind of his flesh,

19. and not holding fast the head, from whom the whole body, being supplied and held together through the joints and ligaments, grows [with] the growth of God.

20. If you died with Christos from the elements of the world, why, as living in [the] world, do you submit to decrees:

21. "Do not handle, do not taste, do not touch" —

22. which things are all for destruction in the using — according to the commandments and teachings of men?

23. Which things have indeed a word of wisdom in self-made worship and humility and harsh treatment of the body, [but are] not of any value against the indulgence of the flesh.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.8 — "the elements of the world":** ta stoicheia tou kosmou — same phrase as Galatians 4:3, 9. "Elementary principles," "elemental spirits," or "cosmic elements." The Colossian situation suggests spiritual powers or astrological forces.

2. **v.9 — "all the fullness of the deity bodily":** pan to plērōma tēs theotētos sōmatikōs — theotēs (deity/divinity) is stronger than theiotēs (divine quality). The entire being of God dwells in Christ's body. sōmatikōs — "bodily/corporally" — may mean "in bodily form" or "in reality" (vs. shadow).

3. **v.14 — "the handwriting in the decrees":** to cheirographon tois dogmasin — a cheirographon is a handwritten IOU or debt document. The "decrees" (dogmata) may refer to the Mosaic law's requirements. God cancels the debt by nailing the document to the cross.

4. **v.15 — "having stripped the rulers":** apekdysamenos — middle voice: "having stripped off from himself." Christ strips the cosmic powers from himself (they clung to him on the cross) and parades them as defeated captives.

5. **v.18 — "worship of the angels":** thrēskeia tōn angelōn — ambiguous genitive: (a) worship directed toward angels, or (b) the worship that angels perform (in which humans participate). Both readings address the Colossian error.

— EMET, AI Translator
